summary: Support deserializing ObjectId from non self-describing formats
description: https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/264

summary: Eliminate redundant clones
description:
Currently, {{CommandResponse::body}} takes in self by reference, requiring a clone of the underlying response document for deserialization. In many cases this clone is unnecessary however, since the {{CommandResponse}} is discarded after the call to {{body}} anyways. As a performance improvement, {{body}} could instead take ownership of {{self}} and avoid the clone. In cases where the response would need to be used again, it could be cloned before calling {{body}}. Alternatively, we could introduce two variants of {{body}} depending on if cloning is desired.

{{execute_operation}} also makes some redundant clones of the already constructed {{Command}}, further degrading performance.

{{Insert::build}} requires cloning of the to-be-inserted documents, but references to the documents could be stored instead to prevent the unnecessary clone.

We should eliminate all these redundant clones to improve the performance of the driver.

summary: Quote keys in Document's Display implementation
description: The {{Display}} implementation for {{Document}} currently formats the output with unquoted keys. This is at odds with {{serde_json}} and the shell print documents, however, which do quote their keys. We should update the implementation to match.

summary: Move trait bounds to implementation instead of Collection struct
description:
Right now, we currently include trait bounds on the type that {{Collection}} is generic over at the struct level. However, we could consider moving these bounds to the implementation blocks instead. This would enable finer tuned restrictions over the functionality that a collection is capable of.

For instance, if a user want to perform a find with a projection, the type that models that projected output would need to be both {{Serialize}} and {{Deserialize}}, though it's only used for reading and would never be inserted directly into the db. If we instead gated the {{impl}} block that contained {{find}} and friends only by {{Deserialize}}, a user could create a collection over that projected type and would only need to implement {{Deserialize}} on the type. Similarly, for write-only situations, users would only need to implement {{Serialize}} on their type, though this is probably less common.

This also has the nice side effect of structs that wrap a {{Collection<T>}} not having to also inherit its trait bounds.

Suggested by [~univerz] here: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/261#issuecomment-858088695]

summary: Use "hello" command when API Version is declared
Use contains instead of iter for error label check
description: The contains_label method on the Error type currently uses an iterator to check for the presence of a label. The storage of labels was updated to use a HashSet in RUST-830, so we should update contains_label as well to use HashSet::contains to avoid iterating the entire set.

summary: DateTimes between the year 10,000 and 99,999 cannot be deserialized
Disable writes on snapshot sessions
Raise client error for snapshot sessions on <5.0 servers
Require passing Stable API options to getMore and transaction-continuing commands
Lossy integer conversions performed in From<_> impls for Bson
description:
The {{From<u32>}} implementation for {{Bson}} performs a simple cast to {{i32}}, which will overflow and return negative numbers for large {{u32}} values. This is also true for the {{From<u64>}} implementation. We should fix the {{u32}} one to convert to an {{i64}} as necessary and perhaps remove the {{u64}} one altogether, as we cannot guarantee it will convert losslessly.

*Acceptance Criteria*
 * Fix the u32 by casting to i64 when needed.
 * Since there is no i128, we can't fix u64 in the same way, so we should remove it since it is data lossy.
 * Add in a try block for the u64 case.

*Open Questions*
 * How do we handle the backport? Is panicking a sensible option? Is deprecation the right option? 
 ** Could panic safely if we also add the dep warning.

summary: Check that explicit sessions were created on the same client as a database/collection
description: The explicit sessions specification indicates that when an explicit session is provided to an operation on a database/collection, it should be verified that the session was created on the same client as the database/collection. We currently do not include that check in any of our {{*_with_session}} methods.

summary: Crash when deserializing/serializing documents that contain decimal128
description: When the {{decimal128}} feature flag is not enabled, attempting to deserialize from a {{Document}} via serde crashes the BSON library if the source {{Document}} contains a {{Decimal128}}. In the driver, this means that iterating a cursor with a decimal128 in the results will crash a user's application.

summary: ObjectId::new only generates 3 bytes of random data instead of 5
Remove decimal128 feature flag and functionality
description:
The {{decimal128}} feature flag enables the usage of the [decimal|https://crates.io/crates/decimal] crate to provide some added experimental / unstable functionality to our {{Decimal128}} type. However, the {{decimal}} crate is backed by the {{decNumber}} C library, [which uses the "Densely Packed Decimal" encoding for the coefficient|http://speleotrove.com/decimal/dncomps.html]. MongoDB, on the other hand, uses "binary encoding" (BID) (see [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/795]). Thus, any BSON generated by the {{bson}} crate that contains decimal128 values will be invalid if the feature flag is enabled. The driver will be able to decode it, but other drivers / MongoDB clients will decode invalid values (see [https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/282)].

There does not seem to be an existing crate that implements the IEEE 754-2008 standard using binary encoding for the coefficient, so we don't have a drop-in replacement to fix this. There is a work-in-progress implementation here that we should pick up once we do RUST-36, but in the meantime, we should just remove the feature flag and any code associated with it to prevent users from accidentally working with bad data.

summary: Avoid going through hex string when deserializing ObjectId from raw bytes
description: The raw BSON deserializer currently represents an {{ObjectId}} via its extended JSON format, which formats the bytes as a hex-encoded string. We could improve performance by instead representing the bytes as a borrowed buffer, similar to what we do for generic subtype {{Binary}}. We'll need to keep using a signifier though to differentiate {{ObjectId}} from {{Binary}}, perhaps {{$oidBytes}} or simply re-use {{$oid}}.

summary: Returned futures are very large
description:
The size of the futures returned from our various methods are really large, to the point of triggering stack overflows on Windows in some of our tests. We should investigate how to reduce the size of these futures to avoid that as well as possibly improve performance.

Related: [https://github.com/rust-lang/wg-async-foundations/blob/master/src/vision/submitted_stories/status_quo/alan_runs_into_stack_trouble.md]

summary: directConnection=false should be allowed in loadBalanced=true URIs
New async version feel very slow in comparaison of sync
description:
I upgrade to 0.10 from 0.9, async version of insert_many look more slow. Did you do any benchmark before I make some ?

 

I expected some slow down with async but this look like 3-4 more slow. If this is intended what should I do ? Create more task ?

summary: Very slow sync find
description:
Finding documents with the sync API in Rust is very slow.

 

Actual behaviour:
When executing collection.find() with the exact same parameters in Rust (mongodb::sync subpackage) and in Python3, Rust needs 19.8 seconds to query 250K documents, while Python3 needs only 1.1 seconds.

 

Expected behaviour:

Rust should need slightly less time than Python.

 

Steps to reproduce:

Populate a collection with a few thousand documents, and execute the same find query once in Rust and once in Python3 that returns a few thousand documents as well.

I have created a GitHub repository with sample code and three scripts to reduce reproduction time: https://github.com/RandomApfel/mongo-rust-slow-issue-reproduction

summary: Fix documentation for index management
description:
Two parts to this ticket:
* Mark the {{CreateIndexResult}} and {{CreateIndexesResult}} structs as {{non_exhaustive}}.
* Make sure that the {{IndexModel}} struct fields are all marked {{pub}}.

summary: Move AWS auth behind a feature flag
description: reqwest in itself is a heavy crate. I see only one mention of it at [mongo-rust-driver/http.rs at master · mongodb/mongo-rust-driver (github.com)|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/blob/master/src/runtime/http.rs]. We should move this functionality behind a feature flag so that users who don't rely on AWS auth don't need to pull in reqwest.

summary: Bump rand dependency to 0.8
description:
changelog here: [https://github.com/rust-random/rand/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#080---2020-12-18]

User PR: [https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/pull/290]

summary: Allow Index Management in Sessions
description: Add the {{with_session}} endpoints to the index management API.

summary: Only return index names in CreateIndex(es)Result
description: The response format for the {{createIndexes}} command differs on sharded and non-sharded deployments in a way that makes an ergonomic API difficult to provide. So instead, we should just drop all the fields off of our existing result types besides the created index names.

summary: Load balancing test failure
description:
The {{server_selection::test::in_window::load_balancing_test}} fails every now and then. We should investigate the cause of the failures and try to fix them.

example failure: [https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_rust_driver_tests__async_runtime~async_std_auth_and_tls~auth_and_tls_os~ubuntu_16.04_test_latest_sharded_cluster_patch_3569cc2279c8a357f0bc332df4b8f990acf61939_60ae94bfc9ec444e290e5296_21_05_26_18_34_40]

summary: Perform wire version check when commitQuorum option is provided to createIndexes
description:
The index management spec requires that the driver raise a client-side error if the {{commitQuorum}} option is specified when creating an index on a pre 4.4 server:
{quote}This option is only supported by servers >= 4.4. Drivers MUST manually raise an error if this option is specified when creating an index on a pre 4.4 server. See the Q&A section for the rationale behind this.
{quote}
We should introduce this behavior to the driver.

summary: Remove deprecated StreamAddress type
description: In RUST-760, the {{StreamAddress}} type was deprecated. We should remove it before the 2.0.0 stable release.

summary: Raw BSON serializer should return false in is_human_readable
description:
The raw BSON serializer currently does not implement the is_human_readable method from the {{Serializer}} trait, and the default implementation returns true. This is inaccurate, however, for raw BSON, which is not human readable, and it's also inconsistent with the raw BSON deserializer, which does return false from this method's equivalent. We should update the serializer to indicate that raw BSON is not human readable.

According to the serde docs, changing this value should be considered a breaking change, so we'll need to do this for the 2.0.0 release.

summary: Upgrade bson dependency to 2.0.0
Enable PLAIN auth connection string tests
description: The PLAIN auth connection string tests in {{src/test/spec/auth.rs}} are currently skipped with a TODO for RUST-197, which has already been completed. The test does not pass once enabled, so we should investigate the issue and then enable the tests.

summary: Collection<T> only implements Clone if T implements Clone
description:
Due to a limitation in the {{Clone}} derive macro, a type that derives {{Clone}} will only actually be {{Clone}} if all of its fields and generic parameters are {{Clone}}. For {{Collection}}, this is overly restrictive, since none of its fields actually store any values of the generic parameter. We should manually implement {{Clone}} instead of deriving it to relax this requirement.

See https://stegosaurusdormant.com/understanding-derive-clone/ for more information.

summary: Race between server selection requesting a check and server monitoring can lead to server selection timeout
description:
The {{sdam::test::min_heartbeat_frequency}} test fails every now and then. We should investigate the cause of the failures and fix them.

[Example Failure|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_rust_driver_tests__async_runtime~async_std_auth_and_tls~auth_and_tls_os~ubuntu_20.04_test_latest_sharded_cluster_patch_960a9a239bacbcfecc22fc67809c4f09e903a16a_60c3a589d1fe075950461d39_21_06_11_18_03_54/0?type=T#L1125]

Ran into this error when running a test-latest-sharded_cluster (variant Ubuntu 20.04 Auth TLS with async-std) check on Evergreen.

summary: Add new constructors to Binary for legacy UUID representations
description: In RUST-687, support was added for serializing and deserializing legacy UUIDs via serde helpers. The UUID spec also requires that the {{Binary}} type is able to be constructed according to a specific {{UuidRepresentation}}. We should introduce such constructors to more fully implement the spec.

summary: Avoid redundant allocations in Collection::clone_with_type
description: See https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/464

summary: Use `ServerAddress::parse` for URI parsing
description: We should use `ServerAddress::parse` in URI parsing for consistency.

summary: Cursor returns error if batch size does not divide the result size
description: See https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/482

summary: topology_closed_event_last failure
description: example failure: https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_rust_driver_tests__async_runtime~async_std_auth_and_tls~auth_and_tls_os~ubuntu_20.04_test_4.4_sharded_cluster_patch_5a0192cf1ba11d9e44c453e14f90d32231689166_6160a067850e613cedf3b697_21_10_08_19_47_52/0?type=T#L1228

summary: secondaryPreferred read preference is not forwarded to mongos
Add a load balancer test case for SRV resolving to 0 hosts
description: This ticket was split from DRIVERS-1835, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Hidden Indexes
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    {panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
TBD when the project completes.
{panel}
h2. Description of Linked Ticket
{panel:title=Epic Summary|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#cccccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
h4. Summary

This project implements functionality to allow indexes to be hidden from the query planner.
h4. Motivation

As MongoDB users evolve schemas and tune performance, they are often in a position where dropping an index could improve query performance and reduce collection overhead. However, dropping an index comes with the risk that an application or script may depend on an index for acceptable performance and recreating can take substantial time.

Allowing users to hide an index provides the opportunity to evaluate impact on user workloads. Restoring the index can then be done quickly, without having to undergo the time and expense of a full index build.

Additionally, adding an index can cause a negative change in performance, if the query planner starts using the new index over a preferable existing index. Creating an index that is hidden on build completion allows users to make it visible at a suitable time for evaluation.

Hiding an index only allows for plan selection evaluation and does not reduce the cost of maintenance. Index removal will still be required to reclaim disk space and improve write performance.
h4. Cast of Characters
 * Product Owner: Katya Kamenieva
 * Project Lead: James Wahlin
 * Program Manager: Craig Homa

h4. Documentation

[Scope Document|http://tinyurl.com/vu9jwys]
[Technical Design Document|http://tinyurl.com/tqrq4sa]
{panel}
 

Specs commit: [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/fafb0076e3c2ee8c35497bd1260c1cfc672c4919]
    See DRIVERS-816 for updated details.

summary: Clarify whether maxPoolSize allows 0 option or no
Use SDAM monitoring in auth_error test
description: The {{auth_error}} SDAM test currently delays while waiting for a server to be marked unknown. Once we have SDAM monitoring implemented we should update this test to wait for an SDAM event.

summary: Bump maxWireVersion to 14 for MongoDB 5.1
Private fields, including the original URI, included in ClientOptions debug output
description: The {{Debug}} implementation of {{ClientOptions}} contains its private fields, including URI that the options were generated from, which may contain credentials. We should exclude any private fields from the {{Debug}} output of {{ClientOptions}} to avoid leaking our internal structures and also to prevent any users from accidentally leaking credentials or other sensitive information.

summary: Remove conditional definition of driver modules
description:
In lib.rs, we currently have a macro that only conditionally defines the modules of the driver if only a single runtime is enabled. This was done to allow for a nice compile error message in case a user accidentally enables both. Due to a recent update to docs.rs, there is now a warning on every method that a specific feature flag needs to be enabled.  e.g. for {{Collection}}: https://docs.rs/mongodb/2.0.1/mongodb/struct.Collection.html#implementations

While its nice to present users with a legible compile error when possible, it doesn't seem worth the visual noise it generates in our documentation. We should remove these conditional defines so that the documentation can omit the feature flag warnings again.

summary: Bson::into_relaxed_extjson produces improper JSON for arrays
description: Github user issue: https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/pull/325

summary: Can't serialize/deserialize uuid properly
description: https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/311

summary: Don't include default write concerns in commands
description: The [spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/read-write-concern/read-write-concern.rst#servers-default-writeconcern] indicates that an empty write concern should be interpreted as the server's default. We should update the write concern serialization in our options structs not to send empty write concerns to the server.
